notes: Fault-tolerant transaction-processing computer line (Himalaya series); acquired by Compaq 1997, then HP 2001; still sold by HPE as NonStop systems
Tandem interconnect fabric (basis for InfiniBand 1999 standard); licensed to Compaq, Intel; influence visible in later PCIe/NVMe-oF

vendor: AT&T Bell Labs / The Open Group
notes: Multi-user time-sharing OS; forks include Solaris, HP-UX, AIX, IRIX; largely displaced by Linux in enterprise
Microsoft enterprise/server OS kernel line; became Windows 2000/XP/Server 2003 and all modern Windows
